name: 1/3–2/3 Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The unit interval [0,1] with its total measure = 1, closed under the partition operation

VINIT.justification: Before any partition is applied, the interval exists as pure potential—no division into thirds yet exists

TANCH.justification: The closed boundary containing the entire system; total measure = 1 is the hermetic seal

AFWD.justification: Directed transformation: [0,1] → [0,1/3], [1/3,2/3], [2/3,1]; growth toward explicit division

AREV.justification: Descent/coagula: the three regions reconstitute back into the undivided whole; parity flip from divided to unified

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: partition operation followed by coalescence operation; the cycle of division and reunion

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the theorem itself as self-referential identity
IMSCRIB.justification: The 1/3–2/3 theorem is self-recognizing: it states that any partition of [0,1] into two parts must have one part ≤1/3 or ≥2/3; the theorem knows itself

FSPLIT.justification: One interval [0,1] branches into exactly two parts: A (measure a) and B (measure 1−a); the core split of the theorem

FFUSE.justification: The two parts A and B fuse back into [0,1]; FFUSE(FSPLIT([0,1])) = [0,1] exactly—the Frobenius constraint holds

EVALT.chosen_element: affirmative branch: one part has measure ≤1/3
EVALT.justification: True evaluation: the theorem's positive case is satisfied; success state in the T-arm

EVALF.chosen_element: negative branch: one part has measure ≥2/3
EVALF.justification: False evaluation: the complementary case; if one part is ≤1/3, the other is ≥2/3; F-arm captures the dual

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the boundary point where measure = 1/3 or 2/3 exactly
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: at the threshold, both conditions hold simultaneously—one part is exactly 1/3 (≤1/3 AND ≥1/3) and the other is exactly 2/3 (≤2/3 AND ≥2/3); both states held without resolution

IFIX.justification: Permanent, append-only: once a partition is measured and recorded (e.g., 'partition A has measure 0.28'), it cannot be undone; the measurement is fixed in the log

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: partition [0,1] into two parts A and B

phase_3.void_description: 00 — the unmeasured continuum before any partition is defined; no division exists
phase_3.true_description: 01 — affirmative state: a partition has been made and one part satisfies the theorem (measure ≤1/3 or ≥2/3); success
phase_3.false_description: 10 — negative state: the complementary part; if one is ≤1/3, the other is ≥2/3; the dual outcome
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: at the exact threshold (measure = 1/3 or 2/3), both conditions hold simultaneously; the boundary is held without resolution

steps.domain_action: unmeasured continuum [0,1] exists as pure potential before any partition
unit interval [0,1] with total measure = 1 is the closed boundary containing the entire system
forward partition operation divides [0,1] into three regions: [0,1/3], [1/3,2/3], [2/3,1]
the partition branches [0,1] into two complementary parts A (measure a) and B (measure 1−a)
evaluate the T-arm: does part A have measure ≤1/3? If yes, the theorem is satisfied (affirmative branch)
evaluate the F-arm: does part B have measure ≥2/3? This is the complementary case (negative branch)
at the boundary point where a = 1/3 exactly, both EVALT and EVALF hold simultaneously—paradice state
reconstitute the two parts A and B back into [0,1]; the partition is reversed
reverse morphism: the divided state descends back into the undivided whole; coagula
compose the forward partition and reverse coalescence into a single cycle: divide, evaluate, reconstitute
the 1/3–2/3 theorem is self-referential—it recognizes itself as the identity law governing all partitions
record the partition outcome permanently: 'partition A measured at 0.28, part B at 0.72; theorem satisfied'—append-only, irreversible

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The measure-theoretic axioms (Lebesgue measure on [0,1]) translate the partition intention into concrete division operations
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Measure values communicate between the two branches via the complementarity constraint: measure(A) + measure(B) = 1
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The partition state is stored as a pair (a, 1−a); the theorem's truth value is cached as a boolean flag (≤1/3 or ≥2/3)
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The partition operation is deterministic and atomic; evaluation follows immediately; reconstitution is synchronous with the reverse operation
phase_5.alfs_store: The canonical reference is the Lebesgue measure on [0,1] and the theorem statement: 'For any partition of [0,1] into two parts, one part has measure ≤1/3 or ≥2/3'

phase_6.cycle_cost: Zero information cost per cycle: the partition operation is reversible (FSPLIT/FFUSE satisfy μ∘δ = id); no entropy is generated
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: System state: [0,1] undivided, measure = 1, no partition recorded
phase_6.post_cycle_state: System state: [0,1] reconstituted, measure = 1, partition outcome recorded in append-only log (IFIX), but the interval itself is restored
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative because FFUSE(FSPLIT(x)) = x exactly; the partition and reconstitution are inverse operations. The only irreversible step is IFIX (the measurement record), which is a one-way append to the log, but this does not alter the interval itself. The domain's measure is preserved.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → FFUSE → AREV → CLINK → IMSCRIB → IFIX
-- Class: 1/3–2/3 Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 7)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [6] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [7] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑗  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [8] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑙 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [9] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [10] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑠 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def 1_3_2_3_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (unmeasured continuum [0,1] exists as pure potential before any partition)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (unit interval [0,1] with total measure = 1 is the closed boundary containing ...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward partition operation divides [0,1] into three regions: [0,1/3], [1/3,2...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (the partition branches [0,1] into two complementary parts A (measure a) and B (measure 1−a)) / FFUSE [7] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (evaluate the T-arm: does part A have measure ≤1/3? If yes, the theorem is sat...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (evaluate the F-arm: does part B have measure ≥2/3? This is the complementary ...)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (at the boundary point where a = 1/3 exactly, both EVALT and EVALF hold simult...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [7]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [7] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitute the two parts A and B back into [0,1]; the partition is reversed)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism: the divided state descends back into the undivided whole; c...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑠)  -- [9] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose the forward partition and reverse coalescence into a single cycle: di...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [10] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the 1/3–2/3 theorem is self-referential—it recognizes itself as the identity ...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the partition outcome permanently: 'partition A measured at 0.28, part...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def 1_3_2_3_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (1_3_2_3_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def 1_3_2_3_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (1_3_2_3_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem 1_3_2_3_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
